chunks.text: Sarah Bergbreiter engineers submillimeter-sized robotic systems - EECS at Berkeley EECS alumna Sarah Bergbreiter (M.S. ’04/Ph.D. ’07) is the subject of a profile by the A. James Clark School of Engineering at the University of Maryland celebrating female engineering faculty during women’s history month. Sarah is an Associate Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering and Institute for Systems Research, and the Director of the Maryland Robotics Center in charge of both the Micro Robotics Lab and the multi-user Robot Realization Lab. She has received PECASE, NSF CAREER, and DARPA Young Faculty Awards, and has also been named one of 25 women in robotics you should know about.
